NLH DATM — winning-essay dashboard

Recipe
Explore atoms
Rhetorical moves
N=3 winners. Atoms 18/8/10 carry citation-apparatus / date noise — discount them. Theme-arc and over-index are signals triangulated from the three winners against the journal baseline, not a validated formula.

The archetypal NLH essay

Every essay averaged — themes by share of corpus mass. Click any bar to inspect that atom.

What a Cohen winner adds

Winner-centroid proportion ÷ corpus mean. ≫1× = over-weighted by the three winners.

The beat arc

Dominant atom per positional segment (opening→close), with each winner's located rhetorical moves beneath.

Gaps

Pick an atom on the left.

Winning move-categories (over-index, winners vs corpus)

The 10 extraction categories (a fixed taxonomy, not clusters). Over-index ≫1 = winners over-invest there. The moves themselves don't form discrete types (they're a continuum) — so this category signal is the one aggregate that holds: winners win on openings and structure, not thesis-construction.

The beat-sheet (opening → close)

Each stage = the move-categories most common there, with the winners' actual labeled moves flagged 🏆.

Every essay by rhetoric (all 288, winners first)

The continuous layer. Click any essay → its labeled move-sequence and its nearest kin by rhetorical profile (cosine on move embeddings). This is what reshape_essay.py runs on.
Pick an essay on the left.
